programmes.name: Bachelor of Administration specialising in Public Administration and International Relations
programmes.notes: Mathematics minimum is level 3; Mathematical Literacy minimum is level 4. Rule encoded at level 4 (stricter) — students with Mathematics level 3 should confirm eligibility with the faculty.

programmes.notes: Covers Grades 7 to 12. Elective modules in Physical Sciences or Life Sciences additionally require Physical Sciences level 5 and Mathematics level 5.

programmes.name: Higher Certificate in Sports Sciences [1 year]
programmes.notes: Selection based on sports achievements in consultation with TuksSport; preference given to nominees of official UP sports clubs.

programmes.name: Higher Certificate in Sports Sciences [2 year online]
programmes.notes: Online programme; students must have at least part-time access to schools, sports clubs or accredited training facilities.

programmes.notes: Mathematics or Accounting at level 5 accepted. Physical Sciences requirement uncertain from PDF extraction — verify against official faculty brochure.

programmes.notes: Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results.

programmes.notes: Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results.

programmes.notes: Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Radiography in Diagnostics
programmes.notes: Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Clinical Medical Practice
programmes.notes: Also referred to as BCMP. Physical Sciences or Life Sciences accepted. Selection based on Grade 11 results and Biographical Information Form.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Medicine and Surgery
programmes.notes: Also referred to as MBChB. Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results. Applicants from countries with their own medical schools cannot apply.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Sports Science
programmes.notes: Physical Sciences or Life Sciences accepted. Selection based on Grade 11 final examination results. BSc Honours in Sport Science or Biokinetics required to qualify as sports scientist or biokineticist.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Speech-Language Pathology
programmes.notes: Only 50 students admitted per year; selection based on academic achievement; conditional selection process starts in August.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Audiology
programmes.notes: Only 50 students admitted per year; selection based on academic achievement; conditional selection process starts in August.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Information Design
programmes.notes: Online portfolio required by 30 June alongside UP application. Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission confirmed with APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Work
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12. Departmental selection in second year includes aptitude test and interview with a minimum average of 60% in social work first-year modules.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts specialising in Languages
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Science specialising in Industrial Sociology and Labour Studies
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Social Science in Heritage and Cultural Sciences
programmes.notes: Option: Heritage and Cultural Tourism. Possible name change to Bachelor of Social Science specialising in Heritage and Cultural Tourism. Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Music [4 year]
programmes.notes: Music: level 4 (50–59%) or Grade VII Practical + Grade V Theory (UNISA/Royal Schools/Trinity). Practical audition (15 min, three style periods, min 60%) and online theoretical test (min 60%) required. Final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Music [5 year]
programmes.notes: Foundation 5-year programme. Music: level 4 or Grade V Practical + Grade III Theory; audition (10 min, min 50%) and theoretical test (min 50%) required. Admitted students may not apply for any other degree programme except Bachelor of Music.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Drama [3 year]
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12. Admissions take place in August.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Drama [4 year]
programmes.notes: Foundation 4-year programme; first year is a foundation year; audition required. Applicants who meet the 3-year programme criteria must apply for that programme instead.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts specialising in Philosophy, Politics and Economics
programmes.notes: Students with APS 32, Mathematics level 4, and English level 5 may be admitted to another degree first and transfer after passing STC 110 and STC 122.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Political Science specialising in International Studies
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Political Science specialising in Political Studies
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Fine Arts [4 year]
programmes.notes: Portfolio and competency test (conceptualisation, drawing, interview, A4 portfolio) required with 60% in each component. Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12. Admissions in July and September.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts in Fine Arts [5 year]
programmes.notes: Foundation 5-year programme. Competency test (conceptualisation, drawing, interview, A4 portfolio) required with 50% in each component.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts specialising in Visual Studies
programmes.notes: Conditional admission based on APS 30 from Grade 11; final admission requires APS 28 in Grade 12.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Laws
programmes.notes: Also referred to as LLB. Applicants are urged to apply as early as possible from 1 April.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Law
programmes.notes: Also referred to as BCom (Law). Also listed in Faculty of Economic and Management Sciences.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Arts specialising in Law
programmes.notes: Also referred to as BA (Law). Also listed in Faculty of Humanities. A BA (Law) degree together with an LLB degree is required to practice as a lawyer.

programmes.name: Bachelor of Theology
Bachelor of Divinity
Diploma in Theology
programmes.notes: Intended for candidates who completed NSC/IEB without required university admission (Diploma Studies endorsement).
